ORDER AND STIPULATION FOR CONTINUANCE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT GRANTED. SUFFICIENT PRIMARY INSURANCE IS AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFF TO SATISFY THE UNPAID BALANCE OF PLAINTIFF'S STIPULATED JUDGMENT. THE EXCESS LIABILITY POLICIES AT ISSUE ARE NOT TRIGGERED, PLAINTIFF HAS NO DUTY TO RECOVER ON THEM, AND DEFENDANT HAS NO DUTY TO DEFEND. THE "UNDERLYING INSURANCE" IS NOT JUST THAT SPECIFICALLY LISTED IN THE UNDERLYING POLICY, BUT "ANY OTHER INSURANCE AVAILABLE TO THE INSURED..." THE "UNDERLYING LIMITS" INCLUDES AVAILABILITY TO THE INSURED IRRESPECTIVE OF BANKRUPTCY OR INSOLVENCY OF ANY INSURER. (SEE EXHIBIT E.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-13-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT NORTH AMERICAN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.
APPEARANCES: Richard T. Ferko, Esq. of Law Office of Richard T. Ferko 818/712-2222 for pltf & opposing party (appearing via CourtCall); A. Carl Yaeckel, Esq. of Grimm, Vranjes, McCormick & Graham LLP 619/231-8802 for deft & moving party; Florence H. Gerlitz, Esq. of Haight Brown & Bonesteel LLP 310/215-7100 for deft General Security National Insurance Company (appearing via CourtCall); Benjamin R. Larson, Esq. of Selman Breitman 979-0400 for deft Scottsdale (appearing via CourtCall).
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for summary judgment granted. Sufficient primary insurance is available to plaintiff to satisfy the unpaid balAnce of plaintiff's stipulated judgment. The excess liability policies at issue are not triggered, plaintiff has no duty to recover on them, and defendant has no duty to defend. The "underlying insurance" is not just that specifically listed in the underlying policy, but "any other insurance availAbLe to the insured..." The "underlying limits" includes availability to the insured irrespective of Bankruptcy or insolvency of any insurer. (See Exhibit E.) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
